title: Kalakand (Milk Cake)

ingredients.title: Ingredients
items: Paneer (freshly made or soft store-bought) – 250 g
Rose water / Kewra essence – ½ tsp (optional, for luxury aroma)
Saffron strands – a few, soaked in 1 tbsp warm milk
Edible silver leaf (varak) – for restaurant-style finish

instruction.title: Prepare Paneer (if making fresh)
steps: Boil 1 liter milk, curdle it with lemon juice or vinegar.
Strain through muslin, wash, and press lightly. Crumble and keep aside.

instruction.title: Reduce Milk
steps: In a heavy-bottom kadai/pan, add the remaining 1 liter milk.
Simmer on medium heat, stirring continuously, until it reduces to almost half and turns slightly grainy.

instruction.title: Build the Base
steps: Add crumbled paneer into the reduced milk.
Stir continuously to avoid sticking; cook until it thickens.

instruction.title: Sweeten & Enrich
steps: Mix in condensed milk, sugar, and ghee.
Continue cooking on low heat until mixture leaves sides of the pan and comes together.
Add cardamom powder, saffron milk, and rose/kewra essence.

instruction.title: Set & Garnish
steps: Transfer mixture into a greased tray lined with parchment.
Spread evenly to ~1-inch thickness, smoothen top.
Sprinkle slivered nuts, press gently, and apply edible silver leaf.

instruction.title: Rest & Cut
steps: Allow to set at room temperature for 1–2 hours (or refrigerate for 30–45 mins).
Cut into neat squares or diamonds for service.

instruction.title: Professional Presentation
steps: Serve 2 elegant diamond pieces on a slate or minimalist white plate.
Garnish with:
Pistachio dust
A saffron strand
A micro mint leaf or rose petal
Optional: drizzle a light rabri reduction or rose syrup on the side for fine dining plating.

chefTips: Boil 1 liter milk, curdle it with lemon juice or vinegar.
Simmer on medium heat, stirring continuously, until it reduces to almost half and turns slightly grainy.
Continue cooking on low heat until mixture leaves sides of the pan and comes together.
Spread evenly to ~1-inch thickness and allow it to set properly before cutting.
Serve with pistachio dust, saffron strand, and a micro mint leaf or rose petal for restaurant-style presentation.
